meaning: adj.透水的,潮湿的,阴湿寒冷的
examples: People who toiled in dim, dank factories were too exhausted to enjoy their family life.
The kitchen was dank and cheerless.
The basement in our house is dark and dank, so it is full of mold.
Cold fronts whip the Duirinish and Trotternish peninsulas, giving Skye a dank, mysterious personality.
The wrong orientation can ruin the courtyard's purpose, making it look cold and dank.
